timeline_html:
Summary. Nadia has experienced long‑standing shedding that began over a year ago and is still gradually worsening. Several overlapping factors have been at play, including postpartum‑type hormonal change, emotional stress, and a recent medication adjustment.

Your hair-loss triggers.

  • Postpartum‑related shift (resolved; Nov 2025–Jan 2026)
  • Major emotional stress (ongoing; started Jan 2019)
  • Medication change (ongoing; started Jun 2026)

Timeline forecast. Based on the postpartum window, the main shedding peak would have occurred around Mar–Apr 2026, with stabilization expected between Jun and Oct 2026 and early baby‑hair regrowth from Jul 2026 onward. Because emotional stress and medication effects are still active, these dates may extend slightly. Dominant driver: the ongoing emotional stress, which has persisted for years and overlaps with more recent hormonal and medication changes, is likely keeping your follicles in an extended shedding phase.

Where you are now. You appear to be in the early regrowth phase—shedding should start to calm while new hairs emerge. Keep supporting your scalp health and stress recovery; steady improvement usually follows within a few months. 🌱
